hints.text: Road lines **all white OR all yellow, never mixed**: all-white most common; all-yellow in national parks / high-snowfall areas
hints.uniq_note: All-yellow lines are essentially Chile-only in the Americas; all-white also occurs in Argentina.

shared_with.note: Argentina has a decent amount of all-white-lined roads.
shared_with.diff: Yellow-black chevrons + square poles → Chile; white-red chevrons → Argentina.

hints.text: Gen3: **solid white car w/o antenna** — plain, or w/ a visible **black section (unique to Chile)**; Gen4 = blue car

shared_with.note: Plain white antenna-less Gen3 car there too.

shared_with.diff: Black section → Chile; red-brick towns → Bolivia.

hints.text: **Square concrete poles w/ an indent on both sides** + small pinholes up top (fewer horizontal supports than Brazil's ladder poles); wooden poles fairly common too

shared_with.note: Brazilian ladder poles look similar but have more supports in the indent.
Brazilian commercial plates are red — can look similar in some light.

hints.text: **Slots at the top of signposts**; chevrons **yellow w/ black arrows** (Argentina: white-red)
Bollards Spanish-style (white front reflector / yellow-orange back) but rare; more often the **orange temporary bollard w/ two white top stripes**; bridges often have **red-painted fence posts**
4000+ km long → **climate = latitude**: arid desert north, Mediterranean centre (Santiago), cold wet oceanic far south (Köppen map)
**Road naming: first letter runs A→Y from north to south** — extremely useful; 2-digit **area codes** regionally memorable (provinces differ in the centre)

bullets: Atacama (northern third): extreme desert, often zero vegetation (extends into Peru/Bolivia/NW-Argentina)
Arica y Parinacota: smooth sandy eroded/bulbous mountains + valleys
Rocky uneven mountains: Atacama + N-Coquimbo; brown-red soil in Atacama (Gen3)
Around Santiago: semi-arid dry hills, scattered vegetation
Bío Bío: radiata pines w/ puffy needles; narrow tree-lined roads (also Araucanía)
Far-E Araucanía: monkey-puzzle trees (also across the border in Argentina)
Isla Grande de Chiloé: rolling NZ-like farmland + Chilean rhubarb (giant cabbage look)
S-Los Lagos/N-Aysén: lush green valleys, rocky mountains, overcast
S-Aysén: autumn colours in much coverage; dotted road lines → Los Ríos & Aysén

hints.text: **Recognisable roads**: A-31 = high altitude w/ steep valley drop north + Gen3 sunset; Ruta 21 (Calama→Chiu Chiu) = Gen4 sunset w/ pink-glowing volcanoes; B-710 & Ruta 5 = more Gen4 sunset; **Ruta 1** = coast squeezed between mountain wall (east) & Pacific (west); Ruta 41 = valley + snowy peaks + triple yellow lines; purple lupines → Ruta 240; **all-yellow lines on concrete → Magallanes** (barren grassy treeless far south)

**Nature parks**: Lauca = snow-capped line + 2 symmetrical lone volcanoes, fresh dark roads; Pampa del Tamarugal = Tamarugo trees along a NW road; Alto Loa = salt pans → snowy volcanoes; Los Flamencos = smooth reddish mountains + salt pans + triple yellow; Nevado Tres Cruces = red sand, salt pans, mountain pass; Llanos de Challe = brown dirt + juniper + overcast (Pan de Azúcar similar, barer); Alto Bío Bío = treeless cold + triple yellow + monkey puzzles east; Puyehue = dense vegetation, tar snakes, dead trees east

**Cities**: Arica = gentle sandy ridge; Iquique = towering ridge; Antofagasta = rocky uneven ridge; San Pedro de Atacama = clay-brick walls w/ triangle tops; orange street signs → Lanco (+ Malalhue); Villa O'Higgins = big mountains right east + orange-stained wood everywhere

**Lamp & pole city metas**: orange lamps → Calama & Chañaral; green dome lamps → Copiapó; photocell lamps → Santiago; grey flat-top lamps → Talca; blue lamps w/ yellow stripe → Concepción; silver-grey lamps → Puerto Montt & Temuco; pole paint: white w/ black number → Arica, yellow → other Atacama cities, black round → El Salvador

**Special cars & trekkers**: 'Outback' trailer behind the car → south of Santiago zoo; grey Hilux w/ snorkel → Baños Morales; white truck w/ mirrors + antenna → Conguillío NP; white pickup → **Puerto Williams** (southernmost covered town, far from all other coverage); ALMA radio telescopes (2 trekkers); horse trekker → NE of San Felipe (Argentina has a similar one!); Torres del Paine = glacier walk w/ orange helmets
